target.name: Hailo
target.founder: Orr Danon, Hadar Zeitlin, Avi Baum

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $336M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Hailo is a Series D semiconductor chip company that has raised $336M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 11 private and 3 public peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including same-stage companies like SambaNova Systems ($5.0B) and Tenstorrent ($2.7B), as well as adjacent-stage peers like DEEPX ($529M).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $336M total raised proxy, implying a $504M valuation. Peer-median analysis uses three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (SambaNova, Tenstorrent, DEEPX), which yield a $2.7B median valuation with a range of $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied without Hailo's disclosed revenue figure.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $428M to $3.1B, with a median of $1.6B. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty: the stage-progression method yields a lower bound based on capital deployed, while the peer-median approach pulls toward the higher end driven by SambaNova's $5.0B valuation. Key uncertainties include Hailo's relative competitive position within its peer cohort, revenue trajectory, and whether the stage-progression multiplier appropriately reflects current market conditions for Series D semiconductor companies.
